rubric_item_scores.evidence_summary: Verified by parsing artifact previews: weighted components (driver_term_*) match 0.35/0.25/0.15 multipliers; weighted_sum and margin values recompute correctly for sampled rows; warn_threshold equals 0.08*largest_driver_value; sensitivity_shift columns are present and computed per methods note.
rubric_item_scores.failure_mode: missing_per-record_provenance_columns; no separate runnable workbook/script artifact provided; methods_note lacks explicit tie-breaking rule for equal weighted drivers (edge-case requirement).
rubric_item_scores.improvement_suggestion: Add explicit per-record provenance columns in the CSV that cite the exact embedded-packet field keys or parameter names used for each threshold/limit decision. Provide a runnable notebook or script artifact (Jupyter notebook or script) that reproduces the CSV end-to-end (currently only a code snippet is included). In methods_note.md, add the required tie-breaking rule for equal weighted_driver cases and explicitly state the warn-rule as 'apply only when margin >= 0 and margin < 0.08*largest_driver' to remove ambiguity.
rubric_item_scores.reasoning_summary: The CSV and methods_note implement the exact margin formula margin = metric4 - (0.35*metric1 + 0.25*metric2 + 0.15*metric3). Intermediate weighted components, weighted_sum, margin, largest_driver, warn_threshold (0.08*largest_driver), per-record qc_verdicts, and sensitivity-shift computations are present and recompute correctly for sample rows (examples R1..R4 match the formula). The verdict mapping used (fail when margin<0; warn when margin>=0 and margin < warn_threshold; pass otherwise) aligns with the intended interpretation of the rubric for non-negative margins, and all records are correctly marked fail given negative margins. Sensitivity-shift analysis and stepwise code snippet are provided in methods_note.md.

rubric_item_scores.improvement_suggestion: 1) Provide an explicit, reproducible runnable script/notebook artifact (not only a code snippet in methods_note.md) that re-runs the CSV generation end-to-end and is attached as a separate deliverable. 2) Explicitly document the tie-breaking rule for largest_driver in methods_note.md per rubric edge-case requirements (e.g., prefer metric name with higher absolute weighted value or a deterministic name-order fallback). 3) Document deterministic numeric rounding rules used for sensitivity-shift calculations so the CSV values can be reproduced exactly (minor numeric discrepancies in margin_plus5/minus5 suggest rounding differences).
rubric_item_scores.reasoning_summary: The CSV and methods_note show the exact margin formula margin = metric4 - (0.35*metric1 + 0.25*metric2 + 0.15*metric3) was applied. Intermediate weighted components (driver_snr_35, driver_event_rate_25, driver_neuropil_15), weighted sum, margin, largest_driver_label/value, warn_threshold (0.08*largest_driver) and per-record verdicts are present for all 4 records. Recomputed values from a sample match the CSV (e.g., R1 weighted components and margin). Verdict logic implemented matches rubric: margin < 0 => fail; 0 <= margin < 0.08*largest_driver => warn; otherwise pass. Sensitivity-shift analysis using sensitivity_shift_pct=5% is included and columns for margin_plus5pct_sensitive and margin_minus5pct_sensitive are present. Methods_note documents mapping of embedded packet metric names and limits and provides script-equivalent logic.
